commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Windows scheduled-task runtime reads to query the task-scoped `schtasks` entry directly, forwards status timeouts through daemon and node service probes, and adds focused daemon/status regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +64, Tests +212. Total +276 across 19 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible rather than locally run here: current main and `v2026.5.28` still perform the broad preflight and omit service-summary timeout forwarding. The PR body adds Windows Crabbox after-fix smoke for the relevant status commands.
commands.review_followup: - No automation-fixable code defect remains; maintainers should apply the normal merge gate and decide whether the supplied Windows proof is enough for this availability-sensitive path.

target.title: fix(daemon): query Windows task runtime directly
target.body:
## Summary
- remove the broad `schtasks /Query` preflight from `readScheduledTaskRuntime()` so Windows status reads go straight to the task-scoped `/Query /TN ... /V /FO LIST` lookup
- preserve Startup-folder fallback behavior when the task-scoped lookup fails
- thread `--timeout` through status service summaries, node-service wrappers, and Windows `schtasks` probes so `status`, `status --json`, and `status --all` do not silently fall back to default schtasks timeouts
- add regression coverage for task-scoped runtime reads, timeout forwarding, node-service forwarding, restart call ordering, and `status --all`

## Linked Issue
- Closes #49187

## Real Behavior Proof
Behavior addressed: Windows status no longer depends on a broad global `schtasks /Query` preflight before reading the OpenClaw Gateway scheduled task runtime, and status timeout options now reach the Windows service probes.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox native Windows, AWS Crabbox Windows WSL2, and focused local Vitest in the maintainer worktree.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.test.ts src/daemon/node-service.test.ts src/commands/status-all.test.ts src/commands/status-runtime-shared.test.ts src/commands/status.service-summary.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks-exec.test.ts src/daemon/service.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts -- --run`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.daemon.config.ts --reporter=verbose`
- AWS WSL2 Crabbox `run_a03985230c25`: focused daemon/status tests plus `openclaw status --json --timeout 3000` smoke on head `06fd757f1c`
- AWS native Windows Crabbox `run_c0f91a1f60bd`: `pnpm check:changed` on the rebased branch before the final no-overlap/test-only rebase, including core typecheck, split-core oxlint, and changed guards
- AWS native Windows scheduled-task smoke `run_cab15d627408`: noninteractive setup, Gateway scheduled task install/start, `status`, `status --json`, `status --json --all`, and `status --all` with `--timeout 3000`

Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output from the WSL2 status smoke and native Windows scheduled-task smoke:

```text
wsl.sha=06fd757f1c
$ openclaw status --json --timeout 3000
wsl.status.json.ok

native scheduled task: OpenClaw Status 51486
$ openclaw status --timeout 3000
exit=0
$ openclaw status --json --timeout 3000
json=parsed ok exit=0
$ openclaw status --json --all --timeout 3000
json=parsed ok exit=0
$ openclaw status --all --timeout 3000
exit=0
```

Observed result after fix: the WSL2 JSON status smoke completed successfully, native Windows status commands completed against an installed scheduled task, and the rebased head `b928832c5cea` passes the focused local status/schtasks tests plus the full daemon Vitest shard that previously failed.

What was not tested: the final head was not rerun through another full native Windows `pnpm check:changed` after the last no-overlap/test-only rebase; exact-head native coverage is represented by the focused local tests plus the prior native full/scheduled-task proof on the unchanged runtime patch shape.

## Review Notes
- No `CHANGELOG.md` update: this is normal PR release-note context, not release changelog generation.
- Autoreview clean after maintainer fixes.
